reason: Frontend spec index for this tracker and all child implementation work.
Feature-first layout rules for vulnerability, phishing-mail, log-analysis, and weak-password surfaces.
Stateful security UI, masked/sensitive display, explicit unavailable/unauthorized branches, and shared primitive rules.
Domain query hook naming, permission gating, query key shape, and sensitive polling rules.
URL state, server state, local state, global ActorContext, and sensitive temporary state boundaries.
Backend contract mirroring policy, zod boundary validation, branded IDs, and no-backend-import rule.
MSW handler layout, schema-validated mock responses, in-memory db reset, and required handler tests.
Forbidden/required UI patterns and test expectations for masking, authorization, and critical metric text summaries.
The tracker mirrors backend contracts into frontend schemas and defines API/MSW boundaries across layers.
The child surfaces must converge on shared patterns without prematurely duplicating or over-abstracting components.
Platform-level authority for roles, sensitive-output restrictions, mail/log/raw-body constraints, and MVP scope.
Parent MVP task context and cross-task sequencing.
Frontend shell, router, providers, MSW, fetch helper, ActorProvider, and shared component baseline established by the prerequisite task.
Tracker PRD defining shared constraints, child list, dependency order, and acceptance criteria.
Child surface PRD for vulnerability list/detail and shared list pattern baseline.
Child surface PRD for mail list/detail, raw-body non-display, and UNAVAILABLE/BODY_ONLY_SIZE_LIMIT branches.
Child surface PRD for redacted log event stream, mandatory asset group query, and textual attack trend summaries.
Child surface PRD for masked weak-password findings, temporary cleartext/export constraints, and one-time password handling.
Current frontend package architecture, scripts, MSW assumptions, routes, and out-of-scope notes from the prerequisite frontend task.
Route registration point for child surfaces and route-level viewer/raw-evidence guards.
MSW in-memory database that child surface fixtures and handlers must extend.
MSW handler barrel that child endpoint handlers must join.
Only frontend network entrypoint and schema validation boundary.
Existing ActorContext and permission helpers for route-level and action-level gates.
Existing shared primitives and state components to reuse before creating new shared UI.
Backend source contract to mirror for vulnerability surface.
Backend source contract to mirror for phishing-mail surface.
Backend source contract to mirror for log-analysis surface.
Backend query/read-model contract source for log events and attack trends.
Backend source contract for weak-password masked findings and report/export flows.
